title: B1.6bn from foreign filmmakers expected
desc: Thailand expects foreign film productions to inject over 1.6 billion baht in revenue next year, following strong interest from film producers at the recently ended Hong Kong International Film and TV Market (FILMART).

title: Senate panel to vote on axeing Cambodia MoU
desc: A special Senate committee will vote on March 24 on whether to recommend revoking Thailand-Cambodia MoU 43, a memorandum of understanding that governs land boundary demarcation, after previously backing the cancellation of MoU 44 on maritime and offshore areas.

title: Pheu Thai settles cabinet picks
desc: Pheu Thai has settled its cabinet nominees, completing all eight allocated posts as government formation moves closer to completion.

title: Activist demands DPM's exit from govt oil supply task force
desc: Activist Srisuwan Janya has asked Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul to remove Deputy Prime Minister and Transport Minister Phiphat Ratchakitprakarn from the government's emergency oil supply task force, alleging conflicts of interest amid a worsening nationwide fuel shortage.

title: Freight and farms suffer fuel blows
desc: Fuel shortages are disrupting farms and freight across Thailand, threatening harvests and supply chains as the government rolls out emergency measures to stabilise supplies.

title: Rising fuel costs threaten prices of essential goods
desc: Rising fuel and transport costs are threatening to drive up prices of essential goods as the Commerce Ministry seeks to stabilise retail prices until April.

title: Energy woes 'will escalate'
desc: Political figures from across Thailand's spectrum, along with consumer advocates, are urging the government to move beyond routine fuel-price management and prepare for a full-blown energy crisis.

title: Judges shelve overseas study
desc: A European study tour for 26 Supreme Court judges has been cancelled following intense public criticism over costs and necessity.

title: Shockwaves in Asia: War, Oil and the Future of Tourism (Part Two)
desc: Rising geopolitical tensions ripple through global energy markets, driving up oil prices and aviation fuel costs – pressuring airfares and dampening long-haul travel demand to Asia, particularly from economically cautious European travellers. BANGKOK, Thailand – The global power shifts explored in Part One do not remain confined to geopolitics. They move quickly—often invisibly – through economic systems. For Asia, and particularly for Thailand, the consequences are both immediate and tangible. The most direct transmission channel is energy. Any sustained disruption in Middle

title: True Corp allays submarine cable concerns
desc: True Corporation has reassured customers that its internet services in Thailand remain stable and unaffected despite potential risks to undersea cable systems stemming from the conflict in the Middle East.

title: K-pop kings BTS rock Seoul in comeback concert
desc: SEOUL - The South Korean megastars BTS reunited on Saturday for their first show in nearly four years, blowing away enormous crowds in Seoul for a K-pop extravaganza livestreamed to millions more worldwide.

title: Pheu Thai cabinet hopeful ‘stressed’ over land case
desc: Pheu Thai Party deputy leader Sudawan Wangsuphakitkosol has admitted feeling “a bit stressed” after learning that the Department of Special Investigation (DSI) had summoned her to acknowledge charges related to alleged land encroachment in Ubon Ratchathani.

title: Matisse’s last years in the spotlight at Paris expo
desc: PARIS - The final years of Henri Matisse’s artistic life, marked by the Nazi occupation of France and a brush with death and surgery, will light up a twilight retrospective opening next week.

title: Jury finds Musk ​misled Twitter shareholders
desc: SAN FRANCISCO - A ‌US federal jury has found Elon Musk liable for claims he defrauded Twitter shareholders by trying to drive down the social media company’s stock price so he could renegotiate or back out of a $44-billion takeover in 2022.

title: People’s Party MP cleared of royal defamation
desc: A People’s Party MP has been acquitted of a royal defamation charge arising from a speech he made at a protest in Ubon Ratchathani province in 2020, according to Thai Lawyers for Human Rights (TLHR).

title: 30-day visa-free stay ‘sufficient’, says minister
desc: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs has confirmed it will propose reducing the visa-free stay for foreign tourists in Thailand from 60 days to 30 days in a bid to curb abuses and mitigate potential security threats.

title: Bigger role planned for SMEs in supply chains
desc: The Finance Ministry is preparing tax reduction measures for large businesses that help bring small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) into their supply chains, in a bid to help upgrade local SMEs.

title: National artist's family opening museum to protect public collection
desc: The family of national artist Chakrabhand Posayakrit will open a museum of his work in Bangkok on March 29, 2026, to keep his paintings accessible to students and the public.  Vallapbhit Sodprasert, chairman of the Chakrabhand Posayakrit Foundation, said the artist refused to sell his work to prevent it ending up in private hands and urged the public to help preserve the...

title: Thai MP acquitted of lèse-majesté charges, from 2020 speech
desc: UBON RATCHATHANI — 20 March 2026, A provincial court has acquitted MP Piyarat Chongthep, also known as “Toto”, of lèse-majesté and related charges over a speech delivered at a 2020 protest, according to court and rights group reports. The Ubon Ratchathani Provincial Court delivered its ruling at about 09:15 dismissing all charges against Piyarat, a party-list MP from the People’s Party and former leader of the We Volunteer (WeVo) group. He had been charged under Section 112 (lese-majeste), Section 116 (sedition) and the Computer Crime Act in connection with remarks made during the “Let the Chi

title: Apple patches security flaws for older iPhones and iPads
desc: Apple has released new iOS updates for older iPhone and iPad models that are no longer eligible for the latest software versions, aiming to address security vulnerabilities and protect user data.

title: EC under the microscope
desc: Allegations of irregularities in the handling of the general election by the Election Commission (EC) have drawn widespread attention in the country’s unfolding constitutional debate.

title: 1.3 million meth pills seized along Mekong in Nong Khai
desc: NONG KHAI — 21 March 2026, Authorities in northeastern Thailand have seized 1.3 million methamphetamine pills worth an estimated 39 million baht (about 1.1 million USD) along the Mekong River, officials said. The drugs were discovered by village security volunteers during a patrol of a high-risk border area in Sangkhom district, provincial officials announced. According to the governor of Nong Khai, the team was patrolling the Mekong riverbank near Ban Pha Daeng village at about 08:30 on 20 March when they found three suspicious packages left by the water. Officials later confirmed the package

title: Seven novice monks allege sexual abuse by former Bangkok temple monks
desc: PATHUM THANI — 21 March 2026, Seven novice monks have come forward with allegations of sexual abuse against two former senior monks from a prominent Bangkok temple, with their parents seeking assistance from the Pavena Foundation for Children and Women. The group requested support in filing complaints with the Anti-Human Trafficking Division (AHTD) on behalf of four additional novices, forming a second group of complainants. Three minors had previously filed complaints. The case follows an earlier development from 26 February 2026, when Ms. Pavena, president of the foundation, accompanied thre

title: Thai election commission defends ballot design amid court scrutiny
desc: Thai election commission defends ballot design amid court scrutiny Legacy Thailand’s Election Commission (EC) has defended its use of barcodes and QR codes on election ballots, insisting that voting remains free, fair, and confidential amid growing scrutiny over the practice. The Constitutional Court is currently reviewing a petition questioning whether these tracking features violate constitutional principles. EC Secretary-General Sawaeng Boonmee said the commission is awaiting a court summons to provide further testimony, reaffirming that all elections are conducted lawfully and by sec

title: Soaring oil prices drive EV demand surge in Korat
desc: NAKHON RATCHASIMA — 20 March 2026, A major electric vehicle (EV) dealer in Korat says tensions in the Middle East and surging global oil prices are driving a sharp rise in EV demand, with bookings increasing significantly. Mr. Sudtirak Phansai-chuea, an executive at Ek Saha Group, said the current spike in global fuel prices and emerging shortages have directly influenced consumer behaviour, forcing many to adapt more quickly than before. He noted a clear surge in interest, reflected in a sharp increase in showroom visits and enquiries about EVs. Compared with late last year, the number of pro

title: Fuel shortage thinning Suvarnabhumi taxi ranks
desc: A fuel shortage is severely disrupting taxi services at Suvarnabhumi Airport, with large SUV taxis gradually suspending operations and only about 2,500 vehicles currently in active service, out of a fleet estimated at more than 5,000, operators say.

title: Python found coiled around Buddha statue in Nakhon Nayok
desc: NAKHON NAYOK — 21 March 2026, A three-metre python was safely removed after it was found coiled around the head of a Buddha statue inside a temple hall, local rescue workers said. The incident occurred at about 10:30 at the viharn of Luang Pho Phra Phuttha Samrit Sitthichok in Mueang district, after the site’s caretaker alerted authorities. Rescue teams responding to the call found the large python, weighing around 10 kilogrammes, wrapped around the head of the statue, which has been enshrined in the hall since 1989. Using climbing equipment, rescuers accessed the back of the statue and carefu

title: Chinese national arrested in Bangkok for police impersonation scam
desc: Chinese national arrested in Bangkok for police impersonation scam Legacy A Chinese national has been arrested at a Bangkok hotel on suspicion of impersonating a police officer to extort millions of baht from Thai victims, following the earlier arrest of an accomplice. Police from Chachoengsao province, acting on a court warrant, detained the suspect in his room at a hotel in the Huai Khwang district yesterday. Officers seized 5,484,200 baht in cash, three land title deeds, and two vehicles for further investigation. The operation was led by deputy superintendent Suradech Nakmuak and Police Ca

title: Thai influencer sentenced for online gambling promotion
desc: Thai influencer sentenced for online gambling promotion Legacy A 24 year old social media influencer has been sentenced to six months in prison for promoting an illegal online gambling website to her nearly one million followers. Narumon “Mind” was found to have used her Facebook account, verified by Meta and boasting over 740,000 followers, to advertise a gambling site through her story posts in exchange for significant payments. The account also featured dance videos in revealing outfits alongside the gambling links. She was charged with directly or indirectly promoting gambling.

title: Newborn’s body found in Bangkok shopping mall restroom
desc: Newborn’s body found in Bangkok shopping mall restroom Legacy A newborn’s body was found discarded in a rubbish bag inside a shopping mall restroom on Sukhumvit Road, Khlong Toei, Bangkok, on Thursday. Police are reviewing CCTV footage and questioning witnesses as part of their investigation. The incident was reported at 4.24pm on March 20. Police Lieutenant Prem Chamnandu, a deputy investigator at Thong Lo Police Station, was alerted and proceeded to the scene with forensic officers and members of the Ruamkatanyu Foundation. Upon arrival, police found a cleaner and a security guar

title: 曼谷刷屏！千人沉浸式观影，《镖人》把中国武侠带火到了泰国
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 3月19日，由泰华新媒体协会、泰国头条新闻联合主办，泰国最大医美集团SLC冠名赞助、六福珠宝为主赞助的中国电影《镖人：风起大漠》泰国特别观影活动，在曼谷One Bangkok One Ultra Screens盛大启幕。千名嘉宾齐聚一堂，沉浸式感受中国武侠的硬核魅力。 活动现场嘉宾云集、氛围热烈，在泰华人商协会，包括泰国中华总商会、泰华进出口商会、泰国中国企业总商会、泰国中国和平统一促进总会、泰国华人青年商会、泰国香港总商会、泰国福建商会、泰国中华会馆、泰国北京总商会、泰國蘇商總會、泰国浙江商会、泰国贵州商会、泰国四川会馆、泰国深圳总商会、泰中侨商联合会、泰国江西总会（总商会）、泰国莆仙总会、泰国石狮商会、泰国泉州晋江联合会、泰国闽商商会、泰国澄海同乡会、泰国桂商总会、泰国广西总商会、泰国无锡商会、泰国江浙沪总商会、泰国江苏商会、泰国温州商会、世隆华商联谊会、泰国南通商会、泰国海南会馆的代表，中资企业与品牌代表，泰国知名艺人Punpun Sutatta、Doraepond Thanakorn、Kaownah Kittipat、Indy Thanadol、Ryuki Napat、Team Tatchanon、Cream Soung、August Achita、Noey Samita、Pancake Purimpath、Lemon Vanisa、Alexandria

title: Tuscan Wine Tasting at Dicey Reilly’s Avani Pattaya Resort 21 March 2026
desc: Tuscan Wine Tasting at Dicey Reilly’s Avani Pattaya Resort 21 March 2026. Sip, savour and discover the elegance of Tuscany at an exclusive Frescobaldi Wine Tasting Night at Dicey Reilly’s. Join us for a refined evening celebrating the artistry of Frescobaldi , featuring one premium white and two exceptional red wines. Each pour is perfectly paired with a specially curated buffet crafted to complement every glass. Date : 21 March 2026 Time : 6:30 pm -8:30 pm Price : THB 1,850 net per person Venue : Dicey Reilly’s, Avani Pattaya Resort For bookings, please email at pattaya@avanihotels.com or cal

title: 泰国加速抢投资！1700亿项目全面落地在即
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，3月20日，泰国投资促进委员会（BOI）召开会议，跟进大型项目投资进展，重点推动Thailand FastPass快速审批机制落地，并着力解决电力供应与投资用地等关键瓶颈问题。BOI秘书长纳立在会后通报了相关进展。 会议通报了Thailand FastPass措施的推进情况。该系统旨在加快大型目标产业项目的审批与许可流程。首阶段入选的16个项目总投资额超过1700亿泰铢，目前均已获得投资促进批准，其中7个项目已提交投资促进证申请，另有4个项目已获得相关合作单位的许可或批准，其余项目正在持续跟进中。 此外，会议还跟进了2023年至2025年期间获批的78个大型项目，总投资额约4800亿泰铢。其中35个项目（约1000亿泰铢）已启动投资，30个项目（约1100亿泰铢）计划在2026至2027年启动投资，另有13个项目（约2700亿泰铢）仍面临电力、土地及许可等问题。预计若相关问题得到解决，到2030年将可额外带来约3500亿泰铢的实际投资。 会议重点讨论了两大核心问题的解决方案，包括电力与清洁能源以及投资用地供给。 在电力与清洁能源方面，BOI认为有必要加快基础设施准备，以支持高科技产业及数据中心项目的发展。目前已有55个数据中心项目获得投资促进，总用电需求超过4.5吉瓦，未来还将显著增长。会议已指示能源监管委员会协调BOI、电力机构及相关部门，共同制定电

title: Fuel shock hits Pattaya as island and fishing trip operators face soaring costs
desc: Passenger ferries heading to Koh Larn continue to depart from Pattaya packed with tourists, even as operators warn that surging fuel prices are putting heavy pressure on their operating costs. PATTAYA, Thailand – Rising global oil prices linked to tensions in the Middle East are beginning to impact the tourism sector in Pattaya, as passenger boat operators report mounting fuel costs that are increasingly difficult to absorb. The ongoing conflict involving Iran and Israel has pushed energy prices upward in international markets, creating ripple effects for businesses that rely heavily on fuel,

title: Motorists say ‘Price doesn’t matter, just let us fill up’ as fuel queues stretch in Sri Racha
desc: Motorists line up for more than a kilometer to refill their tanks at a Thai Oil fuel station in Sri Racha, Chonburi, after word spread that the station was allowing fuel purchases without strict limits. PATTAYA, Thailand – Long lines of motorists stretched for more than a kilometer outside a fuel station operated by Thai Oil in Sri Racha on March 20, as drivers rushed to refill their tanks after learning the station was allowing fuel purchases without strict limits. The station, located in front of the Thai Oil Refinery in Thung Sukhla, saw heavy traffic from early morning as residents and tra

title: 泰国拟调整免签政策：60天或缩短至30天
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，3月20日，泰国外交部长西哈萨透露，外交部正牵头审议签证政策，拟将外国游客免签停留期限从60天缩短至30天，以平衡旅游发展与国家安全需求。该提案将在进一步完善后提交政府审议。 西哈萨表示，此前赛塔政府时期为促进旅游业发展推出了60天免签政策，虽然旅游业对国家经济增长至关重要，但当前政策审议委员会认为60天的停留时间可能过长。实际上，对于一般游客而言，30天的停留时间已能满足旅游需求。如游客确有需要，仍可申请再延长30天停留。 他指出，这一调整并非针对任何特定国家或国籍，而是出于对政策效用的综合考量。较长的免签期限可能被一些并非以旅游为目的入境的人员利用，甚至有人借此长期停留而不申请相应类型的签证。部分涉案人员正是利用长期免签政策进入泰国，并进一步流动至周边国家开展与旅游目的不符的活动。 西哈萨强调，泰国仍将高度重视接待和服务外国游客，致力于为游客提供便利。与此同时，也有必要维护国家安全，堵住政策漏洞，防止免签政策被用于从事与旅游目的不符的活动。 （编译：Juliette；审校：WAN；来源：matichon） The post 泰国拟调整免签政策：60天或缩短至30天 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: Clash among transgender groups over missing phone draws crowds of tourists at Walking Street Pattaya
desc: Two groups of transgender individuals confront each other near the entrance of Walking Street Pattaya early on March 20 over a dispute involving a missing mobile phone. PATTAYA, Thailand – Tensions flared early Thursday morning when two groups of transgender individuals gathered to settle a dispute over a missing mobile phone near the entrance to Walking Street Pattaya in Pattaya, attracting a large crowd of curious tourists. At around 3:30 a.m. on March 20, reporters were alerted by motorcycle taxi drivers stationed near the popular nightlife area in South Pattaya that more than 30 transgende

title: Foreign man found naked and shouting on Phra Tamnak Road in Pattaya, police seek to identify relatives
desc: Authorities detain a foreign man found walking naked and shouting along Phra Tamnak Road in Pattaya early on March 20. PATTAYA, Thailand – A late-night disturbance in Pattaya caused alarm among residents after a foreign man was found walking naked and shouting along a roadside before authorities stepped in to detain him. At around 1:00 a.m. on March 20, officers from the Tourist Police Bureau together with municipal enforcement officers from Pattaya City responded to reports of a foreign man behaving erratically along Phra Tamnak Road near the entrance of Soi Phra Tamnak 5 heading toward the P

title: Classical music evening at Ben’s Theater Jomtien on March 26
desc: The performance will feature acclaimed musicians Eri Nakagawa on piano and Omiros Yavroumis on violin. PATTAYA, Thailand – Classical music lovers in Pattaya will have a special opportunity to enjoy an evening of chamber music when Ben’s Theater Jomtien presents a live concert at 8 p.m. on Thursday, March 26, 2026. The performance will feature acclaimed musicians Eri Nakagawa on piano and Omiros Yavroumis on violin, both associated with the Mahidol University College of Music. The evening’s programme will include works by Robert Schumann, César Franck and other composers from the cl

title: German tourist injured after falling from songthaew on Pattaya Beach Road
desc: A female tourist from Germany receives first aid after reportedly falling from a songthaew on Pattaya Beach Road on March 20. PATTAYA, Thailand – A female tourist from Germany was injured after reportedly falling from a songthaew (shared pickup taxi or baht-bus) along Pattaya Beach Road on March 20, causing alarm among people at the scene. The incident occurred along the busy beachfront road, where the tourist reportedly lost her balance and fell from the vehicle. Witnesses said she suffered a leg injury that appeared visibly deformed, prompting immediate concern from bystanders. Officers from

title: Pattaya reviews city development plan, sets long-term vision for quality urban growth
desc: Deputy Mayor Manot Nongyai chairs a Pattaya City Development Committee meeting at City Hall on March 19 to review revisions to the city’s development plan and outline long-term strategies for the future of Pattaya. PATTAYA, Thailand – City officials in Pattaya have moved forward with reviewing the city’s development strategy as part of efforts to strengthen long-term planning and guide the city toward sustainable growth and improved quality of life. The meeting of the Pattaya City Development Committee was held on March 19 and chaired by Deputy Mayor Manot Nongyai. City executives, members of

title: Warng Warng Taiwanese Shabu: just 30 minutes drive from Pattaya
desc: Warng Warng Original Taiwanese Shabu near Phala Beach in Ban Chang, a family-owned eatery offering a refreshing alternative to the busy Pattaya and Jomtien dining scene. BANCHANG, Thailand – Warng Warng Original Taiwanese Shabu is a family-owned restaurant near Phala Beach, Ban Chang, a refreshing alternative to the familiar Pattaya and Jomtien scene. Shabu was originally a Japanese hotpot of thinly sliced meat and stirred vegetables boiled in water and served with various dipping sauces. But the Taiwanese version uses less oil and is generally regarded as healthier as well as tastier. (

title: Severe weather warning issued for 40 Thai provinces
desc: Severe weather warning issued for 40 Thai provinces Legacy Thailand’s Meteorological Department has warned of severe weather tomorrow, with thunderstorms, hail, and strong winds forecast across 40 provinces, with the northeast among the worst-affected regions. Over the next 24 hours, the upper part of Thailand will experience hot daytime weather. Rainfall is set to increase in the northeast and east, with thunderstorms, strong winds, and occasional hail forecast for the lower north, northeast, central, and eastern areas.
